definitions: To say goodbye to someone at the start of a trip (e.g., at an airport or station).
To defeat an opponent in a competition or contest.
To deal with or get rid of something troublesome or unwanted.
examples: We went to the airport to see them off.
The team managed to see off their rivals in the final.
She quickly saw off the last of the cake.
He saw off the intruders with a stern warning.
